tree_topic: Arts & Entertainment > Online Media > Online Image Galleries
questions: Identify the digital art and design publication that released a feature listicle titled 'The 17 best photography websites'. After identifying the publisher, search their archives to locate a specific buying guide dedicated to mobile photography hardware. Provide the exact, full headline of this mobile guide, which distinguishes itself by explicitly stating in the title that the devices were 'tried and tested by a photographer'.

Locate the specific article titled "The 17 best photography websites" published by the digital media outlet "Creative Bloq". Based on this specific source, provide the complete list of all 17 recommended websites in the order they are presented.

logic_chain: The best camera phones – tried and tested by a photographer

After identifying the publisher, search their archives to locate a specific buying guide dedicated to mobile photography hardware. Provide the exact, full headline of this mobile guide, which distinguishes itself by explicitly stating in the title that the devices were 'tried and tested by a photographer'.

It then demands Wide information retrieval to locate a secondary article within that publisher's ecosystem, filtering for a specific title string ('tried and tested by a photographer') found in the source text.

rounds.answer_b:
The digital art and design publication that released "The 17 best photography websites" is digital photography school. Their mobile photography hardware buying guide featuring "tried and tested by a photographer" in the title is titled "The 10 best phones for photography 2023: tried and tested by a photographer" [1] [2].

## References  
[1] Digital Photography School. "The 17 best photography websites." Accessed 2023.  
[2] Digital Photography School. "The 10 best phones for photography 2023: tried and tested by a photographer." Accessed 2023.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed to provide a correct or useful response. 

Agent A experienced a technical failure, outputting only a citation marker "[1]" with no text or references, rendering it completely useless.

Agent B provided a coherent response but failed the **Deep Logic** check. It incorrectly identified the publisher as "Digital Photography School" (the correct publisher for the article "The 17 best photography websites" is **Creative Bloq**). Consequently, the specific buying guide title it retrieved was also incorrect/hallucinated to fit the wrong publisher. 

Since both agents failed to identify the core entity and provide the correct information, this is a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A failed completely to generate a response, providing only a bracketed number '[1]' with no text or content. Agent B successfully identified the source, extracted a list of 17 websites, and formatted them clearly with a citation. Although the tail end of Agent B's list contains some questionable entries (likely due to scraping specific portfolio examples from the source article rather than just platforms), it provided a functional, helpful, and formatted response, whereas Agent A provided nothing.
